description_long: This is a closed rectangular egg carton made of molded paper pulp, designed to hold a dozen eggs. The carton is primarily white with a green and yellow printed label on the top, featuring branding, product information, and images of chickens and grass, suggesting free-range eggs. The top surface has two circular ventilation holes and a printed Casino logo, along with nutritional and barcode details on the side. The carton has a textured, matte finish with visible seams and molded contours for structural support. The bottom section is black and shaped to cradle individual eggs securely, with six rounded compartments visible from the side. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative patterns beyond the printed label.
description: A white and black molded paper egg carton with a green and yellow printed label, designed to hold a dozen eggs. The carton features ventilation holes, branding, and a textured, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the top label with branding, two ventilation holes, and the Casino logo prominently displayed.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the closed lid and the molded contours of the carton, with minimal label detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the nutritional information, barcode, and the back edge of the carton, with the black egg compartments visible below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the closed lid and the molded contours, similar to view 1 but from the other end.
attribute_orientation: lying flat with the label facing upward
attribute_contextual_placement: Most likely found in a kitchen or grocery store, used for storing and transporting eggs.
